dimension_notes.naming_quality: Clean naming: BaseModel, Miner, ImplicitRecommender follow class hierarchy. NumPy-style docstrings everywhere. Minor: u_col/i_col abbreviated in private methods.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Split improves clarity: each submodule has a single concern. _convert_to_orig_type dispatch chain is still a long if/elif in _miner.py but now contained in one focused file.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Literal types on format params. TYPE_CHECKING imports used correctly. from_transactions still accepts Any for data param rather than a union type.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: IMPROVED: _result and _grouped_result now explicitly initialized in Miner.__init__. _check_fitted pattern consistent. Some methods still accept broad Any params.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: Consistent ValueError/TypeError hierarchy. Specific error messages with context. Minor: some ImportError handlers silently fall back.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: IMPROVED: Split model.py into 4 focused submodules. _type_utils.py centralizes type coercion. _compat.py still uses string-based type detection.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: SIGNIFICANTLY IMPROVED: model.py monolith addressed — split into _mixins.py (195L), _base.py (240L), _miner.py (330L), _recommender.py (310L). Helper extraction completed.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: IMPROVED: Clear 4-file structure mirrors class hierarchy. __init__.py re-exports maintain backward compat. Each submodule has single responsibility.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: IMPROVED: Miner methods isolated in _miner.py. Recommender methods isolated in _recommender.py. _prepare_transactions/_fit_transactions overlap still present.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Improved: consolidated duplicate imports in _convert_to_orig_type. Remaining: try/except polars import pattern in _miner.py and _recommender.py.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: IMPROVED: model/ package establishes clear dependency flow: _base.py -> _miner.py, _base.py -> _recommender.py. _mixins.py is standalone. No circular deps.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: IMPROVED: _result initialized in __init__. Factory pattern clean in each submodule. Minor: ALS still has 15+ init params.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: Consistent verbose:int, seed:int, NumPy docstrings. Minor: import_optional_dependency imported inline in some methods.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Clean optional dependency management. Core deps (numpy/scipy) separate from optional (pandas/polars/spark). No stale pins.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: 767 tests pass. Hypothesis property tests. Benchmark separation. Minor: viz.py, optuna.py, grouped.py lack dedicated tests.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: Consistent API: miners (mine/predict/association_rules), recommenders (fit/recommend_items/recommend_users). Literal format params. Aliases consistent.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: N/A — computational library with no auth.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: fit_transactions deprecated with DeprecationWarning. recommend_items on RuleMinerMixin has DeprecationWarning. Some old format params partially migrated.
dimension_notes.package_organization: IMPROVED: model/ package with __init__.py clean re-exports. 4 focused submodules instead of 1 monolith. Each public module (als.py, bpr.py) has one class.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Unified: Rust for compute, Python for API. model/ split maintains this philosophy. Zero-copy data handling consistent across all paths.

issues.summary: Private method variables use abbreviated names (u_col, i_col, u_data, i_data) reducing readability
evidence: _recommender.py L98-99: u_col = user_col or str(cols[0]), i_col = item_col or str(cols[1])
issues.suggestion: Rename to full names in _prepare_transactions: user_column, item_column

issues.summary: _convert_to_orig_type in _miner.py uses a 4-branch if/elif chain for type dispatch
evidence: _miner.py L77-115: if pyarrow / elif polars / elif spark / else pandas dispatch
issues.suggestion: Refactor to a dispatch dict or strategy pattern mapping type strings to converter callables

issues.summary: from_transactions accepts data: Any instead of a constrained union type
evidence: _base.py L27: data: Any in abstractmethod signature; _miner.py L112: more specific but still includes Any
issues.suggestion: Create DataFrameLike type alias in _type_utils.py for TYPE_CHECKING annotations

issues.summary: Several public methods accept Any for parameters that have known types
evidence: _base.py from_spark df: Any could be pyspark.sql.DataFrame under TYPE_CHECKING
issues.suggestion: Add TYPE_CHECKING imports for pyspark types and use in signatures

issues.summary: Some ImportError handlers fall back silently without logging
evidence: _miner.py L113: except ImportError: pass in Spark conversion path
issues.suggestion: Add logging.debug for caught ImportError to aid debugging

issues.summary: _compat.py still uses __name__ string comparison for type detection instead of isinstance
issues.suggestion: Use try/import with isinstance for cleaner type detection

issues.summary: Type detection logic (pyarrow/polars/spark check) duplicated between _miner.py __init__ and from_transactions
evidence: _miner.py L57-64 and L147-156: identical _type.__name__ checks for orig_df_type
issues.suggestion: Extract _detect_dataframe_type() helper to eliminate this duplication

evidence: _recommender.py: _fit_transactions calls _prepare_transactions then .fit() — two private methods for one operation

issues.summary: mine_grouped in _miner.py handles 3 DataFrame types with separate code paths (~100 lines)
evidence: _miner.py L213-283: Spark path, Polars path, Pandas path each with separate grouping logic
issues.suggestion: Extract each path into a private method: _mine_grouped_spark, _mine_grouped_polars, _mine_grouped_pandas

issues.summary: try: import polars pattern still repeated in _miner.py and _recommender.py
evidence: _miner.py L228: try: _pl = import_optional_dependency('polars'); _recommender.py L96: same pattern

issues.summary: Import dependency graph is implicit — lazy imports prevent circular deps but make flow unclear
evidence: __init__.py re-exports from 4 submodules; _miner.py and _recommender.py both lazy-import from rusket._dependencies
issues.suggestion: Add a dependency graph comment or diagram to model/__init__.py

issues.summary: ALS.__init__ accepts 15+ parameters creating tight constructor coupling
evidence: als.py: factors, regularization, alpha, iterations, seed, verbose, cg_iters, use_cholesky, use_eals, eals_iters...
issues.suggestion: Group related params into dataclasses: SolverConfig, EalsConfig

issues.summary: import_optional_dependency imported inline in some method bodies rather than at top
evidence: _miner.py L70: from rusket._dependencies import import_optional_dependency inside method body
issues.suggestion: Consistent: either always at module level or always at function top

issues.summary: Optional dependencies lack version bounds in pyproject.toml
issues.suggestion: Add minimum version bounds based on tested compatibility

issues.summary: viz.py, optuna.py, grouped.py lack dedicated test coverage
evidence: No test_viz.py, test_optuna.py, test_grouped.py files exist
issues.suggestion: Add smoke tests for each optional module

issues.summary: recommend_items means cart recommendations on RuleMinerMixin but user recommendations on ImplicitRecommender
evidence: _mixins.py L189: deprecated recommend_items; _recommender.py L153: abstract recommend_items
issues.suggestion: Fully deprecate RuleMinerMixin.recommend_items and document the distinction

issues.summary: fit_transactions still present in _recommender.py despite being deprecated
evidence: _recommender.py L64-77: fit_transactions emits DeprecationWarning but still implemented
issues.suggestion: Consider removing after sufficient deprecation period or documenting removal timeline

issues.summary: _type_utils.py at package root serves model/ subpackage — could move into model/
evidence: _type_utils.py imported by model/_mixins.py, model/_miner.py — primarily used by model package
issues.suggestion: Consider if _type_utils.py should be part of model/ or stay at root as general utility

issues.summary: export_factors defaults to pandas while batch_recommend defaults to polars
evidence: _recommender.py: format='pandas' default; als.py batch_recommend: format='polars' default
issues.suggestion: Standardize on one default or make configurable at model level
